sector.name: Small Finance Banks
sector.description: SFBs recovering as secured lending mix rises

fyPerformance: Gross advances stood at ₹8,687 Cr (+21% YoY); deposits stood at ₹10,018 Cr (+20% YoY)
FY26 NII stood at ₹463 Cr (+13% YoY); FY26 non-interest income stood at ₹99 Cr (+16% YoY)
FY26 PPOP stood at ₹223 Cr (+19% YoY); FY26 PAT stood at ₹141 Cr
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹919 Cr (+20% YoY); Q4FY26 NII stood at ₹122 Cr (+19% YoY)
Q4FY26 NIM stood at 4.06% vs 4.01% QoQ; FY26 NIM stood at 4.04%; FY26 ROA stood at 1.23%
highlights: Capital SFB remains focused on secured, granular and relationship-led lending
Around 98% of the loan book is secured, with 89% of non-corporate portfolio collateralised by immovable property or bank FDRs
MSME / business segment was the key growth driver, growing 46% YoY and 9% QoQ
GNPA stood at 2.54% and NNPA stood at 1.24%; credit cost stood at 0.26% for Q4FY26
CASA stood at 34.7%, while retail deposits remained above 90% of deposits
53% of current high-cost term deposits are due for repricing in the next 2 quarters
Capital Small Finance Bank Ltd.quote: "Capital SFB is outperforming the sector on secured book quality, low credit cost, deposit granularity and asset quality."

fyPerformance: Gross advances stood at ₹36,289 Cr; deposits stood at ₹35,784 Cr (+23% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹140 Cr, with ROA of 1.3% and ROE of 13%
Gross NPA stood at 2.33%, Net NPA stood at 0.87% and SMA stood at 3.66%
Q4FY26 net credit cost stood at ₹156 Cr and gross credit cost stood at ₹192 Cr
Secured asset book grew 28% YoY and secured asset mix stood at 72.6%
highlights: Management said Q4FY26 clearly showed that MFI stress is now behind the bank
Bank met Q4 guidance on PAT, credit cost and SMA
SMA at 3.66% is lower than March 2024 level of 3.99%, showing better exit quality than pre-stress period
Net credit cost declined to 0.47% for Q4FY26 and management expects it to sustain
Secured disbursement in Q4FY26 was the highest ever at ₹5,372 Cr
Management plans to resubmit universal banking application after audited results
Jana Small Finance Bank Ltd.quote: "Jana is outperforming the sector on credit cost normalisation, secured book growth, MFI recovery and PAT guidance."

fyPerformance: Total business stood at ₹48,276 Cr (+15% YoY); gross advances stood at ₹22,426 Cr (+19% YoY)
Deposits stood at ₹25,850 Cr (+11% YoY); retail deposits stood at ₹23,674 Cr (+9% YoY)
FY26 disbursement stood at ₹42,530 Cr (+103% YoY); Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹12,926 Cr (+88% YoY)
Q4FY26 NII stood at ₹518 Cr vs ₹432 Cr QoQ; Q4FY26 NIM improved to 7.3% vs 6.6% QoQ
GNPA improved to 5.4% vs 6.9% YoY and NNPA improved to 1.8% vs 3.0% YoY
highlights: Management's focus remains on building a granular, diversified and increasingly secured lending portfolio
MARG strategy stands for MSME, Agriculture, Retail and Gold loans and is central to reducing dependence on unsecured loans
Secured lending constituted 78% of Q4FY26 disbursements
Secured portfolio mix improved to 61% vs 53% last year
MARG advances grew 47% YoY and MARG portfolio grew 133% during FY26
FY26 credit cost declined to 4.7% vs 6.7% in FY25
ESAF Small Finance Bank Ltd.quote: "ESAF can outperform the sector if secured transition, asset quality improvement and credit cost normalisation continue."

fyPerformance: Deposits stood at ₹13,994 Cr (+32% YoY), with retail deposit share improving to 86%
CASA ratio stood at 22.6%; FY26 net total income stood at ₹1,458 Cr (+10% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹152 Cr vs ₹115 Cr last year
Commercial vehicle finance portfolio stood at ₹1,819 Cr (+36% YoY)
Mortgage book including micro home loans stood at ₹3,013 Cr (+38% YoY)

guidance.value: Credit on UPI, secured cards, digital MSME loans and deposits

highlights: Management said FY26 was a year of disciplined execution and gradual stabilisation
Q4FY26 was encouraging as collections, borrower behaviour and business momentum improved
Inclusive finance slippages reduced to around ₹74 Cr vs ₹116 Cr QoQ
Current book collection efficiency in inclusive finance moved towards 99.7%
Around 99% of the inclusive finance portfolio is covered under CGFMU
Credit on UPI is emerging as a customer acquisition engine, with more than 90% of onboarded customers having CIBIL score above 725
Suryoday Small Finance Bank Ltd.quote: "Suryoday can outperform the sector if inclusive finance slippages keep falling and FY27 PAT guidance is delivered."

fyPerformance: Gross advances stood at ₹46,165 Cr (+22% YoY and +7% QoQ); deposits stood at ₹46,533 Cr (+8% YoY)
Q4FY26 total net income stood at ₹1,239 Cr (+18% YoY and +9% QoQ)
Q4FY26 NIM improved to 7.29% vs 6.72% QoQ
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹213 Cr (+406% YoY and +136% QoQ), the highest ever quarterly PAT for the bank
GNPA stood at 2.49% vs 2.62% QoQ and NNPA stood at 0.68% vs 0.88% QoQ
highlights: Q4FY26 was a strong quarter across business growth, asset quality and profitability
Gross disbursement stood at ₹7,347 Cr, the highest ever quarterly disbursement
Secured disbursement stood at ₹5,835 Cr, also the highest ever
Non-MFI secured book stood at ₹40,409 Cr, up 21% YoY
Net slippages reduced to 0.79%, lowest in the last 10 quarters
Deposit growth was muted at 8% YoY, which remains the key monitorable
Equitas Small Finance Bank Ltd.quote: "Equitas is outperforming the sector on Q4 profitability, asset quality recovery, disbursement momentum and diversified secured lending."

fyPerformance: Q4FY26 net loss stood at ₹188 Cr due to legacy provisioning
GNPA improved by around 330 bps QoQ to 7.7%
JLG X-bucket collection efficiency improved to 99.7% in March 2026 vs 98.5% in April 2025
Fresh NPA slippages net of recoveries and upgrades reduced to around ₹170 Cr vs ₹710 Cr YoY
JLG share reduced to around 28% of gross loan book, while secured lending share improved to 51% vs 43% last year
Utkarsh Small Finance Bank Ltd.outlook: Management said FY26 was about stability over speed and quality over quantity. Q4FY26 started showing green shoots with better disbursements, lower SMA, lower fresh slippages and higher recoveries. Utkarsh remains in recovery mode, and rerating depends on GNPA improvement, MBBL scaling, higher secured mix and reduction in legacy stress.
highlights: Bank is structurally reducing cyclicality by moving away from unsecured JLG dependence
JLG exposure reduced from nearly 88% in March 2020 to around 28% in March 2026
MBBL grew 122% YoY and 40% QoQ and now forms 27% of micro-banking loan book
Around 70% of microfinance book is covered under CGFMU after including Q4 disbursements
Retail term deposits grew 20% YoY; CASA deposits grew 11% YoY and 13% QoQ
Reverse merger with holding company is expected to complete in the next few months, subject to NCLT approval
Utkarsh Small Finance Bank Ltd.quote: "Utkarsh can rerate if GNPA keeps improving, MBBL scales, secured mix rises and legacy stress reduces."

fyPerformance: Deposits stood at ₹45,668 Cr (+21% YoY and +8% QoQ); gross loan book stood at ₹40,655 Cr (+27% YoY and +10% QoQ)
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹9,811 Cr (+32% YoY and +18% QoQ)
Secured portfolio stood at ₹20,079 Cr (+44% YoY and +13% QoQ), with secured portfolio share at 49.4%
Q4FY26 NII stood at ₹1,092 Cr (+26% YoY and +9% QoQ); Q4FY26 NIM stood at 8.5%
FY26 PAT stood at ₹693 Cr; Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹282 Cr; Q4FY26 ROA stood at 2.1% and ROE stood at 17.2%
highlights: Ujjivan crossed multiple milestones in FY26, with deposits and gross loan book both crossing ₹40,000 Cr
Balance sheet crossed ₹50,000 Cr, retail deposits crossed ₹30,000 Cr and savings accounts crossed ₹10,000 Cr
Housing vertical crossed ₹10,000 Cr, while MSME and FIG crossed ₹3,000 Cr each
RBI returned the universal bank application but acknowledged Ujjivan's ongoing diversification efforts
Bank continues to build a more balanced and diversified loan book
Board approved equity capital raise of up to ₹2,000 Cr to support medium-term growth
Ujjivan Small Finance Bank Ltd.quote: "Ujjivan is outperforming the sector on deposit growth, Q4 profitability, NIM, secured book expansion and operating momentum."
